candidates.title: Wildfire near endangered whooping crane nests sparks scientific opportunity
candidates.description: It's too soon to say what the long-term impacts of earlier wildfires might be on the world’s only natural wild migratory whooping crane flock. But scientists could be a step closer to finding out after a wildfire in Wood Buffalo National Park came near a handful of the endangered species' nests.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A glacial lake in B.C. is at risk of bursting — again. How climate change has raised this risk
candidates.description: The threat of a glacial lake overflowing and potentially sending a torrent of meltwater and debris rushing toward homes has become an annual fear for people living near British Columbia's Place Glacier. But experts says this is an increasing risk as the effects of human-caused climate change hit Canada's glaciers hard.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Changes are coming to auto insurance policies in Ontario. Here's what you need to know
candidates.description: As of July, some benefits that were previously mandatory under standard car insurance policies will be optional. This could drop the cost of premiums for customers who opt out of certain coverages, but insurance experts warn against leaving coverage exposed to risk.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfires prompt First Nation in northwestern Ontario to order vulnerable residents to leave
candidates.description: The remote fly-in community of Kasabonika Lake First Nation in northwestern Ontario is making preparations to evacuate vulnerable residents, as concern rises over numerous wildfires surrounding the community. Close to 60 wildfires are now burning across northwestern Ontario after dozens of new fires were sparked over the weekend.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Brooke Henderson 1 shot back of lead entering final round of Women's PGA Championship
candidates.description: Haeran Ryu shot a 4-under 68 on Saturday to take a one-shot lead over Canadian Brooke Henderson after three rounds of the Women's PGA Championship. The 28-year-old from Smiths Falls, Ont., shot a 3-under 69 at Hazeltine in Chaska, Min.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Chuckwagon racing legend Tom Glass dies at 77
candidates.description: Tom Glass, a monumental figure in Western Canadian sports history, has died after suffering a stroke at 77. As "the face of chuckwagon racing," Glass dominated the sport locally and internationally during the 1980s and 1990s.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hundreds march in Vancouver to oppose planned AI data centres
candidates.description: Hundreds of people took to the streets in Vancouver on Saturday to oppose the construction of two new AI data centres in the city.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Evacuation order issued for Lynn Lake, Man., as wildfire expected to reach town Sunday
candidates.description: As of Friday, an out-of-control wildfire south of the town has quickly grown to 1,600 hectares in size since it was detected on Thursday, according to a provincial fire status report.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canadian Army holds sunset ceremony to deactivate 5th Division
candidates.description: The 5th Division of Canadian Army is transitioning with new structure and missions. A ceremony was held on Saturday in Halifax to commemorate its accomplishments in the Atlantic region.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Nearly 20 years after her murder in Quebec, an arrest in Guinea gives her loved ones hope
candidates.description: Rachelle Wrathmall, 31, was found dead in her Lennoxville home in Sherbrooke, Que., in June of 2007. She had been stabbed to death. The day before she was found, her husband left Canada and eventually returned to his home country.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 66 fires currently burning in the Northwest Territories, evacuation alert remains in place for Fort Simpson
candidates.description: "Thousands of lightning strikes" mean a very busy weekend, says N.W.T. fire. Fort Simpson fire remains main concern, as well as fire near the Alberta border that threatens the highway. High heat and the risk of dry lightning continues for the area around Wood Buffalo National Park, Fort Smith and in the Sahtú and Dehcho.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Refining cobalt in Cobalt, Ont.? That's the plan for this northern Ontario town
candidates.description: A facility near Cobalt, Ont., has been tapped to be the home of North America's first battery-grade cobalt refinery. Projected to be fully operational by late 2027, the plant will import and process mined cobalt — experts say it could bring Canada into an industry dominated by China.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Yukon fans celebrate hometown star Gavin McKenna's draft to the Maple Leafs
candidates.description: For Yukon hockey fans, it was like a dream come true on Friday when hometown hero Gavin McKenna was the first overall pick in the NHL draft.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Summer of unsettled weather expected across the Prairies
candidates.description: It's been a cooler start to summer in the southern Prairies, all due to multiple low pressure systems hovering over the region this month. It's also the cause of unsettled weather, which continues into July.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Former envoy to U.S. doesn't think 'serious Americans' will get involved in Alta. separatism
candidates.description: As Alberta pushes ahead with a fall referendum to decide whether an actual referendum on separating from Canada is worth the effort, Canada's former U.S. ambassador says she doesn't think "serious Americans" will get involved in the debate.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta says pipeline proposal on track for July 1 deadline, as uncertainty hangs over project
candidates.description: As a Canada Day deadline fast approaches for a pipeline submission to Ottawa's major projects office, questions still surround whether the proposal will be ready in time, and if the project is commercially viable.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. launches probe into StubHub over World Cup resale ticket fiasco
candidates.description: British Columbia's attorney general announced an inquiry into online ticketing site StubHub, days after a CBC News investigation that revealed a mass failure by the ticket reseller platform and cancellation of thousands of FIFA World Cup tickets in Vancouver, Toronto and host cities across North America.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: King Charles reveals his income tax for the 1st time, but questions remain over royal finances
candidates.description: There was much interest in the move by King Charles to reveal for the first time how much income tax he pays, but in ways, the numbers that emerged this past week add to the longstanding opaqueness around royal finances.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: France reports 1,000 additional deaths as extreme heat shatters European records
candidates.description: France saw around 1,000 additional deaths last week at the height of its record-smashing heat wave, the country's public health agency said Sunday, as the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned that Europe is now the fastest-warming continent and needs to do more to protect its citizens.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: French police accused of targeting Black and Arab men with discriminatory fines
candidates.description: According to a report released last week by Human Rights Watch, (RE)Claim and the Community Centre for Development and Solidarity, French police systematically target Black and Arab youth, conducting identity checks and issuing on-the-spot fines.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kentucky governor says 4 dead amid flooding caused by heavy rains
candidates.description: Flash flood warnings were in effect Saturday for parts of Kentucky and Indiana amid heavy rainfall, according to the National Weather Service.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Russians are turning on Putin as drones hit Moscow. Does it matter?
candidates.description: After the largest Ukrainian drone offensive on the Russian capital and other strikes on energy infrastructure, confidence in Putin's leadership has seemed to waver among civilians and prominent nationalists alike. But analysts are skeptical whether it is enough to turn the tide.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing 11 people, officials say
candidates.description: The plane belonged to a parachutist school. The pilot and all 10 passengers — five students and five instructors — died in the accident, the prefecture said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran says it has launched retaliatory strikes on U.S. bases in Kuwait, Bahrain
candidates.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said early on ‌Sunday that its navy and ​aerospace forces had launched joint missile and drone ​operations targeting U.S. military sites in ‌Kuwait and Bahrain in response to recent U.S. strikes against Iran.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heat wave in Europe shatters records in Denmark, Switzerland and Czech Republic
candidates.description: Temperatures hit record highs from Switzerland to the Czech Republic and Denmark on Saturday, as a heat wave that baked western European countries this week moves to central and eastern parts of the continent.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: At the 2026 World Cup, Iraqi Shias reconcile celebration with grief during holy month
candidates.description: Soccer fans usually feel upbeat emotions when watching their national team play in a FIFA World Cup for the first time in 40 years. But this year, some Iraqi supporters have had to balance cheering the national team with observing the Shia Islamic month of mourning.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump administration allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to 'trusted' U.S. organizations
candidates.description: Anthropic said on Friday that the U.S. government has allowed it to release its powerful Claude Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model to some "trusted" U.S. organizations, partially reversing an order two weeks ago to suspend access over national security risks.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: California appeals court upholds Weinstein's rape conviction, orders resentencing
candidates.description: An appeals court on Friday upheld Harvey Weinstein's 2022 rape and sexual assault conviction in California, but ordered his trial judge to resentence him.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
candidates.description: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
candidates.description: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
candidates.description: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
candidates.description: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
candidates.description: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
candidates.description: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
candidates.description: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
candidates.description: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
candidates.description: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
candidates.description: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
candidates.description: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
candidates.description: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tech firms are blaming AI for mega device and console price rises
candidates.description: Xbox consoles, Nintendo's new Switch 2 and Valve's Steam Deck are just some of the gadgets seeing price hikes in recent months.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: GTA 6 will launch as download only - does that mean the disc is dead?
candidates.description: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
candidates.description: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
candidates.description: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
candidates.description: Forecasters have suggested that summer is likely to be warmer than average with an increased chance of more heatwaves, as Simon King explains.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Future generations being failed if more trees not planted, experts say
candidates.description: Statistics show the number of trees being planted is increasing, but conservationists say it is not meeting targets.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
candidates.description: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trade tensions shake up Brazil's caipirinha spirit
candidates.description: U.S. tariff pressure is pushing Europe and Brazil closer—opening new global doors for everything from aircraft parts to Brazil's cachaça, the base of the caipirinha.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 'If you are alive, make any noise': Venezuela searches rubble on day 4
candidates.description: Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
candidates.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: New strikes test the Iran ceasefire
candidates.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak
candidates.description: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
candidates.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
candidates.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
candidates.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuelans in Colombia scramble to send aid as earthquakes death toll increases
candidates.description: Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed over 1,400 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: This mindset shift can help you get better at using up your leftovers
candidates.description: Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
candidates.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
candidates.description: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
candidates.description: Enrollment in Affordable Care Act insurance fell much more sharply in the first months of 2026 than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued June 28 at 3:42PM CDT until June 28 at 10:00PM CDT by NWS Amarillo TX
candidates.description: * WHAT...Temperatures reaching values of 105 or greater. * WHERE...Armstrong, Carson, Moore, Oldham, and Potter Counties. * WHEN...Until 10 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Hottest temperatures are mostly present in our terrain features such as the Canadian River Valley or Palo Duro Canyon. If you are in or live in those areas please use extra caution.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued June 28 at 3:42PM CDT until June 28 at 10:00PM CDT by NWS Amarillo TX
candidates.description: * WHAT...Hot temperatures up to 108 degrees expected. * WHERE...Hutchinson and Palo Duro Canyon Counties. * WHEN...Until 10 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Hottest temperatures are mostly present in our terrain features such as the Canadian River Valley or Palo Duro Canyon. If you are in or live in those areas please use extra caution.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 28 at 4:38PM EDT until June 28 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Raleigh NC
candidates.description: At 438 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Bailey, or 15 miles southwest of Nashville, moving southeast at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Wilson, Elm City, Bailey, Middlesex, Sims, Emit, Buckhorn Reservoir, Tar River Reservoir, and New Hope. This includes the following highways... Interstate 95 between Mile Markers 127 and 116. NC Highway 264 between Mile Markers 23 and 38.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Warning issued June 28 at 3:37PM CDT until June 28 at 3:45PM CDT by NWS Louisville KY
candidates.description: The Flood Warning will expire at 345 PM CDT this afternoon for portions of central and south central Kentucky, including the following areas, in central Kentucky, Grayson and Hart. In south central Kentucky, Barren, Butler, Edmonson and Warren. Flood waters have receded. The heavy rain has ended. Flooding is no longer expected to pose a threat. Please continue to heed remaining road closures.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued June 28 at 4:37PM EDT by NWS Raleigh NC
candidates.description: At 437 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Fremont, or 13 miles north of Goldsboro, moving east at 20 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Fremont, Lucama, Stantonsburg, Black Creek, Pikeville, Macclesfield, Saratoga, and Eureka. This includes NC Highway 264 between Mile Markers 45 and 56.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued June 28 at 4:28PM EDT until June 28 at 6:30PM EDT by NWS Wakefield VA
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...A portion of east central Virginia, including the following county, Essex. * WHEN...Until 630 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 428 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 3 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Tappahannock, Rexburg, Mount Landing and Caret. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued June 28 at 4:26PM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
candidates.description: At 426 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Lake Weir, or 7 miles northwest of The Villages, moving north at 5 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts around 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Ocklawaha, Silver Springs Shores, Lake Weir, Belleview, and Summerfield.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued June 28 at 4:24PM EDT by NWS Columbia SC
candidates.description: At 424 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along a line extending from 7 miles northeast of Buford to near Mt Pisgah to near Liberty Hill. Movement was east at 40 mph. ...Boaters on Lake Wateree Should Seek Safe Harbor... HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Camden, Cheraw, Bishopville, Lake Wateree Dam, Pageland, Chesterfield, Westville, Cheraw State Park, Northeastern Technical College, Cassatt, Cheraw Municipal Airport, Lee State Park, Carolina Sandhills Wildlife Refuge, Mt Pisgah, Longtown, Goodale State Park, Mcbee, Jefferson, Ruby, and Patrick. This includes Interstate 20 in South Carolina between mile markers 121 and 124.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued June 28 at 4:16PM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
candidates.description: At 415 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 6 miles southeast of Indian Land, or near Waxhaw, moving east at 30 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Monroe, Weddington, Wesley Chapel, Waxhaw, Mineral Springs, Marvin, Indian Trail, Jaars, and Trinity.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued June 28 at 3:16PM CDT until July 2 at 8:00PM CDT by NWS Louisville KY
candidates.description: * WHAT...For the first Heat Advisory, heat index values up to 106. For the second Heat Advisory, heat index values up to 107 expected. * WHERE...Hancock, Ohio, Butler, Logan, Simpson, and Warren Counties. * WHEN...For the first Heat Advisory, until 8 PM CDT this evening. For the second Heat Advisory, from 11 AM Monday to 8 PM CDT Thursday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: M 4.2 - 27 km WNW of Hope, Alaska
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 4.2. The event occurred near 27 km WNW of Hope, Alaska. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
